extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the sprint after Quick-Step controlled 90% of the stage alone when Sky only briefly contributed on the long climb.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step isolated: controlled all day without assistance except brief Sky work on early climb, revealing sprinter team's burden.
CONSONNI-VIVIANI pre-race coordination suspected across teams when racing center line together mid-stage, suggesting Italian collusion before finale.
Caja Rural initiated lead-out but no team had strength to follow, allowing Quick-Step monopoly despite their isolation throughout stage.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: first Grand Tour win despite 3000m altitude making non-easy sprint, suggesting climbing tolerance exceeded expectations
rider_signals.MORKOV Michael: positive: Danish champion paced successfully despite team controlling alone entire stage, demonstrating depth beyond just Viviani
rider_signals.NITZOLO Giacomo: neutral: challenged Viviani in final but Check team lacked infrastructure to complete lead-out against Quick-Step
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: Bora protected him entire stage and positioned well but chose not to contest sprint despite being present
rider_signals.POSTALBERGER Lukas: negative: attempted motivated solo move at 72kph but Quick-Step easily reimposed control, revealing Bora's backup plan weakness
